qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: attribute_filter sandbox escape permits host command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same root cause as the advisory: `attribute_filter` blocks direct Lua-side attribute access but is bypassed when untrusted Lua calls Python built-in `getattr()`/`setattr()` while builtins remain registered.

selected_finding.title: attribute_filter sandbox escape permits host command execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding matches GHSA-69v7-xpr6-6gjm on the specific incomplete `attribute_filter` enforcement in built-in `getattr()`/`setattr()` paths, and a fresh copied-workspace PoC reached host command execution on pinned `lupa==2.6`.
evidence: The advisory describes incomplete `attribute_filter` enforcement when attributes are reached through built-in `getattr`/`setattr`, enabling sandbox escape and host command execution.
The selected finding identifies the same vulnerable mechanism: untrusted Lua uses `python.builtins.getattr()` to recover filtered dunder attributes while `register_builtins` remains enabled.

Fresh runtime evidence in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_99679a28/.judge/poc_runs/lupa_attribute_filter_sandbox_escape/run.log` shows `DIRECT_ACCESS_BLOCKED AttributeError Access to '__import__' is forbidden`, followed by `PROOF_CONTENT EVOHUNT_FILTER_BYPASS_OK` and `VERIFIED True` after the bypass-driven `os.system(...)` call.

poc_executions.command: python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel && .venv/bin/pip install lupa==2.6 && .venv/bin/python reproduction/exploit.py
poc_executions.observed_effect: Pinned `lupa 2.6` loaded, direct `python.builtins.__import__` access was blocked by `attribute_filter`, then the `python.builtins.getattr()` bypass recovered `__import__`, executed `os.system(...)`, and wrote the expected `EVOHUNT_FILTER_BYPASS_OK` proof marker with `VERIFIED True`.
